description_long: This is a small, timber-framed barn with a steeply pitched, red-tiled gable roof. The exterior walls feature a half-timbered pattern with visible dark wooden beams forming X and cross shapes over tan infill panels. The barn sits on a dark, textured stone or concrete base, and one side has an open, covered entryway with steps leading up to it. The front and rear gables are adorned with a distinctive checkered triangular ornament at the peak. There are no visible windows, and the main door is a large, double, cross-braced style integrated into the timber framing.
description: A compact, half-timbered barn with a steep red-tiled gable roof and a large cross-braced double door. The structure features a stone base and an open side entryway with steps.
description_view_0: The front gable is visible, showing the checkered ornament, cross-braced timber pattern, and the main double door.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the open entryway with steps and the overhanging red-tiled roof.
description_view_2: Rear gable view with a similar checkered ornament and cross-braced timber pattern, but no visible door.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the solid wall under the red-tiled roof, with no entryway or door.
attribute_roof_type: steep gable roof with red clay tiles
attribute_siding_pattern: half-timbered with cross-braced and X-patterned wooden beams over infill panels
attribute_door_style: double hinged cross-braced barn door integrated into the timber framing
attribute_window_arrangement: no visible windows on any side
